summary: Lead accepts the independently reviewed M3 task graph for PR integration. The graph is dependency-ordered, keeps all downstream M3 work in DRAFT, records real systems/QA thread IDs, includes a persisted send_input route evidence record, and changes only planning, message, thread registry, and coordination-state files.

tests_run.command: $failed = $false; Get-ChildItem project/tasks/active/M3-*.json | Sort-Object Name | ForEach-Object { node .agents/skills/monsoon-studio-orchestrator/scripts/taskctl.mjs validate $_.BaseName; if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { $failed = $true } }; if ($failed) { exit 1 }
tests_run.result: All 13 M3 task files validated successfully.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON/DAG/scope validation for all M3 task files, dependency ordering, status counts, historical prerequisite, H-003/H-005 dependencies, exit dependencies, and changed path scope
tests_run.result: M3 DAG/scope ok files=13 statuses={"DRAFT":12,"ACCEPTED":1} changed_paths=19

tests_run.command: node -e thread registry duplicate ID check

tests_run.result: M3-TASK-GRAPH-001 is ACCEPTED; 12 downstream M3 tasks remain DRAFT; all prior M0/M1/M2 tasks remain CLOSED.
Ready candidates are M3-HISTORICAL-CLAIM-PIPELINE-001 and M3-POLITY-VASSALAGE-001; neither has been started.
format:check, lint, typecheck, and architecture:check passed.
Already up to date; lockfile unchanged.

decisions: ACCEPT: M3-TASK-GRAPH-001 may be committed and opened as a PR.
No M3 implementation task is started in this task graph PR.
The next dependency-ready M3 candidates after integration are M3-HISTORICAL-CLAIM-PIPELINE-001 and M3-POLITY-VASSALAGE-001.
risks: This is a planning and orchestration integration only; downstream M3 tasks still require independent writer/reviewer routing before implementation acceptance.
M3-H003-H005-VALIDATION-001 is initial M3 evidence only and must not be represented as final M5 player-study proof.
requested_action: Commit and open the M3 task graph PR. Do not start downstream M3 implementation until this task graph is integrated.
